extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won a chaotic sprint ahead of SAGAN Peter (later relegated for headbutting VAN AERT Wout) and BENNETT Sam after a late-stage breakaway collapse.
tactical_patterns: NTT chased breakaway too early for BOASSON HAGEN, exhausting leadout before final kilometer when sprint trains reorganized.
BENNETT Sam positioned third wheel behind rival team's leadout, exploiting opponents' disorganization rather than relying on own leadout.
VAN AERT Wout attacked from fourth position inside final 200m, nearly stealing win despite no dedicated leadout train.
EWAN Caleb won from far back relying on pure speed after positioning chaos, proving confidence matters when sprint organization collapses.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: won from distance without leadout, showing superior raw speed when sprint disintegrates into chaos
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: relegated for headbutting VAN AERT, desperate tactics suggest declining sprint confidence against younger rivals
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: third despite positioning behind wrong leadout, tactical awareness compensating for missing own train
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: nearly won from fourth position without leadout, raw power almost overcame poor positioning
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: negative: NTT burned leadout too early chasing breakaway, tactical miscalculation left him isolated in finale
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: neutral: late solo move nearly succeeded as peloton hesitated, showing teams still miscalculate chase responsibility
